title: Koanophyllon consanguineum
intro: Koanophyllon consanguineum, also known as the "Mexican copperleaf," is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, including infections and inflammatory diseases. Koanophyllon consanguineum contains bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as in traditional folk medicine, particularly in Mexican and Latin American cultures.
taxonomy: Koanophyllon consanguineum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Koanophyllon, which consists of several species of plants. The family Asteraceae is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family, and it includes a wide variety of flowering plants. Koanophyllon consanguineum is native to the Americas and can be found in various habitats.
